title: Cross-Domain Knowledge Graph and Relationship Explorer Policy
principles: Entities and relationships remain typed, source-aware, and independently inspectable.
Every public relationship requires registered evidence or source references and human approval.
Aliases and external identifiers support reconciliation but never trigger automatic merges.
Temporal validity remains explicit for relationships that change through time.
Traversal and path results describe graph connectivity, not causality, importance, or risk.
Public graph exports preserve evidence, confidence, temporal, visibility, and review metadata.
boundaries: No causation inferred from graph proximity, sequence, degree, or path length.
No automatic entity merging or destructive identifier reconciliation.
No individual tracking, surveillance, targeting, or social scoring.
No military targeting, operational planning, or consequential automated action.
No hidden relationship confidence or unsupported relationship creation.
No public relationship without human approval and evidence or source references.
